original_hypothesis.title: minLiqIncentiveMultiplier Can Exceed maxLiqIncentiveMultiplier via Misconfiguration
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: LiqIncentiveCalculator applies Math.min(value, max) then Math.max(result, min). If governor sets minLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18[mode] > maxLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18, the min overrides the max cap, allowing liquidators to extract more collateral than the protocol intends. There is no validation that min <= max in the setter functions.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Lines: `multiplier_e18 = Math.min(ONE_E18 + incentive_e18, maxLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18); multiplier_e18 = Math.max(multiplier_e18, minLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18[_mode]);` — when min > max, the final value is min (exceeding max).
deployment_assumptions: Governor sets min > max for a mode's liquidation incentive
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: LiqIncentiveCalculator.sol: setMinLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18 and setMaxLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18 have no cross-validation

proof_of_signal.pattern: Math.max after Math.min: `Math.min(value, max)` then `Math.max(result, min)` — min wins when min > max; no validation in setters

preconditions: Governor misconfigures min > max for a mode
Positions in that mode become liquidatable

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: Missing cross-validation between min and max bounds in setter functions
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Math.max(Math.min(value, max), min) = min when min > max, exceeding intended cap

analysis_questions: What is the maximum collateral extraction if min is set to 2e18 (200%) with max at 1.5e18 (150%)?
Would this be exploitable by a liquidator who controls a position they can self-liquidate?
Can this be detected before liquidation occurs?
validation_requirements: Set min > max for a test mode, trigger liquidation, verify excessive collateral extraction
Calculate maximum extractable value under misconfiguration
Test that setter does not validate min <= max

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: Governor accidentally sets minLiqIncentive=2e18 and maxLiqIncentive=1.5e18 for a mode. Liquidators receive 200% of debt value as collateral (instead of capped at 150%), rapidly draining position collateral beyond intended limits. If a liquidator self-liquidates an underwater position, they extract excess value at the expense of the position owner.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Excessive collateral seizure during liquidations, draining positions beyond intended limits
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All borrowers in the affected mode
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Governor can fix by correcting the min/max configuration

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No test for min > max configuration scenario

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Abnormally high liquidation incentives visible in getLiqIncentiveMultiplier_e18 calls

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
